proposedChangeSummary: Update plain-English interpretation to reference ICH Q12 Established Conditions (ECs) framework for post-approval change management.
previousValue: Post-approval changes to drug product manufacturing require prior regulatory approval under relevant jurisdiction-specific change control procedures.
proposedValue: Post-approval changes to drug product manufacturing should follow ICH Q12 Established Conditions (ECs) framework. Changes within pre-defined ECs may qualify for reduced regulatory reporting under Post-Approval Change Management Protocols (PACMPs).
changeReason: ICH Q12 introduces a new framework that reduces regulatory burden for well-characterized changes while maintaining product quality assurance.

proposedChangeSummary: Add reference to PACMP as an acceptable change management approach alongside traditional prior-approval supplements.
previousValue: Significant manufacturing changes require a Prior Approval Supplement (PAS) or Changes Being Effected (CBE) notification.
proposedValue: Significant manufacturing changes require a Prior Approval Supplement (PAS), Changes Being Effected (CBE) notification, or may qualify for a Post-Approval Change Management Protocol (PACMP) under ICH Q12 if pre-agreed with the regulatory authority.
changeReason: Aligning with global harmonization of post-approval change categories.

proposedChangeSummary: Add ICH Q12 as a linked standard in the ENABLE-phase crosswalk for change control and lifecycle management.
proposedValue: ICH Q12 — Technical and Regulatory Considerations for Pharmaceutical Product Lifecycle Management
changeReason: New globally harmonized guideline requires crosswalk linkage.

proposedChangeSummary: Update required control to include Contamination Control Strategy (CCS) as mandated by revised Annex 1.
previousValue: Environmental monitoring program and aseptic processing validation.
proposedValue: Contamination Control Strategy (CCS) encompassing environmental monitoring, aseptic processing validation, personnel qualification, and facility/equipment design review. CCS must be documented and regularly reviewed.
sourceReference: EU GMP Annex 1, Section 2 — Contamination Control Strategy
changeReason: Revised Annex 1 elevates CCS from recommended practice to regulatory requirement.

proposedChangeSummary: Update citation reference from Annex 1 (2008) to Annex 1 (2022 Revision, effective August 2023).
changeReason: Annex 1 was comprehensively revised in 2022. Citation must reference current version.

proposedChangeSummary: Add CCS document as a new required evidence item for sterile manufacturing obligations.
proposedValue: Contamination Control Strategy (CCS) document — must cover facility design, environmental monitoring, personnel flows, material flows, process design, and equipment maintenance.
changeReason: CCS is a new documentation requirement introduced by revised Annex 1.

proposedChangeSummary: Update frequency/timing field to reflect end of DSCSA stabilization period and full enforcement date.
proposedValue: Full enforcement from November 27, 2026. All trading partners must be capable of exchanging EPCIS-format transaction data at the package level.
changeReason: Stabilization period ending — obligation transitions from soft enforcement to full enforcement.

proposedChangeSummary: Add real-time temperature monitoring requirement for radiopharmaceutical last-mile delivery per USP <1083> update.
previousValue: Temperature monitoring during transport with documented excursion procedures.
proposedValue: Continuous real-time temperature monitoring during transport including last-mile delivery. IoT-based monitoring devices required for shipments exceeding 4 hours transit time. Documented excursion procedures with defined action limits.
changeReason: USP <1083> update introduces last-mile monitoring requirements not previously specified.

proposedChangeSummary: Clarify ALCOA+ expectations for cloud-hosted QMS and electronic batch records per MHRA 2026 guidance update.
previousValue: Data integrity must follow ALCOA+ principles (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate + Complete, Consistent, Enduring, Available).
proposedValue: Data integrity must follow ALCOA+ principles. For cloud-hosted QMS and electronic batch records: system access controls must ensure attributability; audit trails must be tamper-evident; data migration must preserve original records; validated backup and recovery procedures must ensure availability and endurance. AI-assisted data review is permitted if human oversight and documented review rationale are maintained.
sourceReference: MHRA GxP Data Integrity Guidance, Section 6 — Electronic Records
changeReason: 2026 revision clarifies cloud and AI expectations not covered in original guidance.

proposedChangeSummary: New obligation: AI conformity assessment documentation for high-risk AI systems used in pharmaceutical manufacturing quality control.
proposedValue: Organizations deploying high-risk AI systems in GMP-critical processes must maintain conformity assessment documentation including: risk management analysis, data governance records, technical specifications, and human oversight provisions per EU AI Act Article 11.
sourceReference: EU AI Act, Article 11 — Technical Documentation
changeReason: EU AI Act creates new category of obligations not previously covered in the compliance matrix.

proposedChangeSummary: Add Procurement as an impacted function for revised Annex 1 supplier qualification requirements.
proposedValue: Procurement must ensure supplier qualification includes assessment of contamination control capabilities per revised Annex 1 requirements for incoming material controls.
sourceReference: EU GMP Annex 1, Section 8.3 — Starting Materials
changeReason: Revised Annex 1 expands supplier oversight requirements beyond previous scope.

proposedChangeSummary: Update DSCSA crosswalk to reflect full enforcement requirements including EPCIS interoperability.
previousValue: DSCSA Section 582 — serialization and traceability (stabilization period)
proposedValue: DSCSA Section 582 — Full enforcement: package-level electronic interoperable traceability, EPCIS data exchange, verification router service integration.
changeReason: Crosswalk must reflect post-stabilization enforcement scope.
